---
title: "Formula grammar"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Formula grammar}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

This developer article documents the supported `drm_formula()` grammar,
including response blocks, distributional-parameter formulae, and validation
rules. `bf()` remains a short alias.

The current planning reference is `docs/design/01-formula-grammar.md`.

Current implemented univariate Gaussian random-effect forms include:

```r
drm_formula(y ~ x + (1 | id), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + (0 + x | id), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + (1 + x | id), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + (1 + x | p | id), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + (1 | id), sigma ~ z + (1 | id))
drm_formula(y ~ x + (1 | id), sigma ~ z, sd(id) ~ x_group)
drm_formula(y ~ x + phylo(1 | species, tree = tree), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(
  y ~ x + (1 | id) + (1 | site),
  sigma ~ z,
  sd(id) ~ x_group,
  sd(site) ~ site_type
)
```

The middle `p` is a group-level covariance-block label. It is not residual
`rho12`, and reserved distributional parameter names such as `rho12` should not
be used as labels. Cross-formula use of matching labels remains future work.

Current implemented bivariate form:

```r
drm_formula(
  mu1 = y1 ~ x1 + x2,
  mu2 = y2 ~ x1,
  sigma1 = ~ x1 + x2,
  sigma2 = ~ x1,
  rho12 = ~ x1 + x2
)
```

The first fitted structured-effect marker is an intercept-only phylogenetic
location effect:

```r
drm_formula(y ~ x + phylo(1 | species, tree = tree), sigma ~ z)
```

`phylo()` uses an ultrametric branch-length tree and adds a tree-structured
Gaussian random intercept to the `mu` predictor. Phylogenetic random slopes,
phylogenetic scale terms, and spatial terms are still planned:

```r
drm_formula(y ~ x + phylo(1 + x | species, tree = tree), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + spatial(1 | site, coords = coords), sigma ~ z)
drm_formula(y ~ x + spatial(1 | site, mesh = mesh), sigma ~ z)
```

`spatial()` will use coordinates or a precomputed mesh to build an SPDE/GMRF
precision. These structured effects are separate from residual `rho12`.

Future bivariate random-effect syntax should be explicit about the distinction
between group-level covariance labels and residual `rho12`:

```r
drm_formula(
  mu1 = y1 ~ x1 + x2 + (1 + x2 | p | ID),
  mu2 = y2 ~ x1      + (1 + x2 | p | ID),
  sigma1 = ~ x1 + x2,
  sigma2 = ~ x1,
  rho12 = ~ x1 + x2
)
```
